name: The Optimal Itinerary
tagline: Yale morning → 3 days in Boston → 3 nights in Montréal → Hadley cottage → Home
vibe: A week that starts with Ivy League ambition, settles into Boston's history and harbor, pivots to Montréal's French-Canadian flair with a Tremblant ski day, and winds down at the Adirondack cottage before heading home.

days.label: Day 1 — Monday, Mar 30

legs.notes: I-95 N. Charge at New Haven Supercharger while walking the campus.
I-95 N through Providence. Top off at Boston Supercharger on arrival.

segments.activity: Depart Hopewell — early Monday start
Yale campus walk — Old Campus, Harkness Tower, Beinecke Library
segments.notes: Charge the car at New Haven Supercharger while you explore. ~$15.

segments.activity: Lunch on Wooster Street — Frank Pepe's or Sally's Apizza
Depart New Haven for Boston
Arrive Cambridge — check in to hotel
Evening walk — Boston Common and Public Garden
Dinner at Faneuil Hall Marketplace — seafood & street performers

days.notes: Quick Yale campus stop — no overnight in New Haven. Get to Boston by mid-afternoon.

days.label: Day 2 — Tuesday, Mar 31
days.theme: Harvard, MIT & Boston Harbor Boat Tour

segments.activity: Breakfast at Sofra Bakery, Cambridge
Harvard campus tour (pre-register at college.harvard.edu)
Lunch in Harvard Square
MIT self-guided tour — Infinite Corridor, Stata Center, Killian Court
Boston Harbor sightseeing cruise — 90 min narrated tour from Long Wharf
segments.notes: ~$30/person. Departs from Long Wharf near the Aquarium. Book in advance at bostonharborcruises.com.

segments.activity: Walk the Seaport District & Harborwalk
Dinner in the North End / Faneuil Hall area

days.notes: Campus tours in the morning, harbor cruise in the afternoon. Book the boat in advance — spring sailings can fill up.

days.label: Day 3 — Wednesday, Apr 1
days.theme: Black Boston + Tourist Boston → drive to Montréal

legs.notes: I-93 N → I-89 N → Autoroute 15 into Montréal. Charge in Montpelier midway. Passport required at the border.

segments.activity: Museum of African American History + African Meeting House
Freedom Trail walking tour — start at Boston Common
segments.notes: 2.5 miles. Free self-guided. Hit the highlights.

segments.activity: Old North Church — 'One if by land, two if by sea'
Late lunch at Dudley Café, Roxbury
Pack up, check out, depart Cambridge for Montréal
Depart Boston for Montréal
Charge + dinner stop in Montpelier, VT
segments.notes: Supercharger ~$15. Grab dinner at a local spot.

segments.activity: Arrive Montréal — check in to hotel

days.notes: Sightsee Boston in the morning, drive to Montréal in the evening. It's a 5-hour drive with a charge stop. Bring passports — border crossing on I-89.

days.label: Day 4 — Thursday, Apr 2
days.theme: Montréal — Old Port, River Cruise & Diaspora Culture

segments.activity: Morning walk through Little Burgundy — Oscar Peterson's neighborhood
Notre-Dame Basilica — stunning neo-Gothic interior
segments.notes: Book tickets in advance at basiliquenotredame.ca. ~$8/person.

segments.activity: Lunch in Old Montréal — Place Jacques-Cartier
Le Bateau-Mouche river cruise — narrated St. Lawrence tour
segments.notes: ~$35/person. Glass-enclosed boat, Paris-style. Departs from Jacques-Cartier Pier in the Old Port.

segments.activity: Jean-Talon Market — open-air market, maple products, cheeses
McGill University campus walk
Dinner — Haitian cuisine at Chez Aline

days.notes: Full Montréal day — Notre-Dame, Old Port, river cruise, and the best market in North America. Book the Bateau-Mouche in advance.

days.label: Day 5 — Friday, Apr 3

legs.notes: Autoroute 15 N → Route 117. Charge car in Montréal before departing.
Same route back. Return to your same Montréal hotel.

segments.activity: Early departure for Mont Tremblant
Arrive Tremblant — pick up ski rentals
segments.notes: Reserve rentals in advance at tremblant.ca. ~$65/person for full package.

segments.activity: Ski at Tremblant! Beginner-friendly runs + village views
segments.notes: Lift tickets ~$140/person spring rate. Excellent beginner terrain.

segments.activity: Late lunch on the mountain
Browse the pedestrian village at the base
Depart Tremblant — drive back to Montréal
Arrive back in Montréal — relax at hotel
Dinner in Old Montréal — farewell evening stroll

days.notes: Day trip to Tremblant — no packing up needed. Drive back to the same Montréal hotel for a farewell dinner in Old Montréal.

days.label: Day 6 — Saturday, Apr 4

legs.notes: Autoroute 15 S → I-87 S (Adirondack Northway). Beautiful drive through the Adirondacks. Charge in Plattsburgh.

segments.activity: Morning hike — Mount Royal Park, Kondiaronk Belvedere viewpoint
Brunch in Little Burgundy, then pack up
Depart Montréal — cross back into the US
Charge + stretch in Plattsburgh, NY
Arrive at the cottage in Hadley, NY
Afternoon at the cottage — relax by Great Sacandaga Lake
Dinner at the cottage or nearby in Hadley/Lake Luzerne

days.notes: Break up the drive home with a night at the Adirondack cottage. No hotel cost — just a relaxing transition day.

days.label: Day 7 — Sunday, Apr 5

legs.notes: I-87 S → I-287 E → I-78 W into NJ. Easy Sunday drive — arrive by mid-afternoon.

segments.activity: Breakfast at the cottage, close up
Depart Hadley for home
Charge + coffee near Albany
Arrive home — trip complete!

days.notes: Easy 3.5-hour drive home. No marathon — you broke it up with the cottage.
